articleSummary: This article presents Regex Denial-of-Service (ReDoS) as a security vulnerability—not merely a performance concern—where specially crafted user input can exhaust CPU or memory via backtracking-heavy regular expressions. The author warns of nested quantifiers, overlapping alternation, and unchecked input on hot paths, and recommends mitigations such as bounding input length, adding timeouts, or using non-backtracking engines. The tone is tutorial-like with a security-focused framing, emphasizing that this issue affects major platforms ( .NET, Node, Python, PERL, Java). The intended audience is developers and security engineers who need to treat regex validation as part of their threat model.
